macro_events.event_summary: TotalEnergies signals strong Q1 earnings driven by elevated oil/gas prices amid a US-Iran war, implying a sustained crude price shock.
primary_drivers: Middle East war risk premium lifting Brent/WTI crude prices
Potential disruption to Strait of Hormuz flows (~20% of global oil) raising LNG and shipping rates
Refining crack spreads widening on supply uncertainty

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream leverage to Brent; every $10/bbl sustained move adds roughly $4-5B to annual earnings. Direct peer readthrough from TotalEnergies' Q1 guide.
Similar integrated upstream exposure as XOM; Permian + international production levered directly to crude price. Peer-correlation trade off TTE commentary.
Pure-play E&P with no refining hedge — highest beta to crude among large caps. US-Iran war scenario drives sustained >$90 Brent which flows nearly 1:1 to FCF.
High-leverage domestic E&P (Permian-heavy) with debt-load that deleverages rapidly on higher oil; Buffett-backed name typically leads tape on Middle East shocks.
Cheniere: US LNG export prices spike if Hormuz/Qatar LNG flows are threatened; European/Asian buyers pay up for US cargoes. Direct beneficiary of Iran war scenario.
Diamondback — pure Permian E&P, extremely high operating leverage to WTI. Will outperform majors on sustained crude spike.
Scorpio Tankers — product tanker rates surge on Middle East disruption as ton-miles extend (rerouting around Hormuz). Direct war premium.
Halliburton — oilfield services benefits from upstream capex response to sustained high prices; US shale activity re-accelerates.

losers.rationale: Delta — jet fuel is ~25% of operating costs and unhedged; sustained crude spike from Iran war directly compresses margins. Most fuel-sensitive major airline.
United — similar jet fuel exposure as DAL, minimal hedging. International routes also face Middle East airspace disruption costs.
Southwest — historically hedged but hedge book has rolled off; domestic-only means no fare offset from international premium cabins. High fuel beta.
Carnival — bunker fuel costs spike; Mediterranean/Middle East itineraries cancelled or rerouted adding further cost. Double hit from war scenario.
FedEx — diesel/jet fuel a major variable cost; surcharges lag price moves by weeks, compressing near-term margins.
Dow — naphtha/ethane feedstock costs rise with crude; chemicals demand already weak so cost pass-through is limited.
Walmart — consumer discretionary spend contracts as gasoline prices surge; historical correlation of retail sales to pump prices is negative and meaningful.

headlines: TotalEnergies Expecting Strong Q1 Earnings Amid US-Iran War

macro_events.event_summary: Wells Fargo issues a bullish long-term gold call suggesting prices could nearly double to $8,000/oz, despite gold just suffering its worst monthly drop in a decade.
primary_drivers: Sell-side price target upgrade reinforcing structural gold bull thesis
Potential dip-buying rotation back into gold miners after sharp monthly drawdown
Operating leverage of miners to gold price — every $100 move in spot translates to outsized FCF changes

beneficiaries.rationale: Newmont is the largest US-listed gold producer; AISC ~$1,500/oz means any move back toward prior highs (let alone $8k) is pure operating leverage to FCF. Most direct, liquid way to express a Wells Fargo gold upgrade.

Barrick Gold — large-cap pure-play producer, highly correlated to gold spot, benefits directly from sell-side price target upgrades driving flow back into senior miners after the worst monthly drop in a decade.
Agnico Eagle — senior producer with high-quality assets, typically one of the first names bid when gold-miner sentiment reverses; leveraged to spot price via low AISC.
Wheaton Precious Metals — streaming model with fixed costs means nearly all gold upside flows to margin; analyst-driven gold rallies historically lift streamers alongside miners.
Franco-Nevada — royalty/streaming pure-play on precious metals prices with no operating cost inflation risk; direct beneficiary of a bullish gold price target.
Pan American Silver has meaningful gold exposure alongside silver; smaller-cap miners show higher beta to sell-side gold upgrades and post-selloff rebounds.
AngloGold Ashanti — high-beta senior producer, historically one of the largest movers on bullish gold calls given operating leverage and prior underperformance.

headlines: Wells Fargo says gold could nearly double to $8,000 an ounce — even after its worst monthly drop in a decade
